strengths: Clear identification of three specific real-world objects with socio-cultural contexts
Consistent alignment of each objectâs commentary to the chosen IA prompt
Sustained justification supported by concrete detail and cited evidence
Purposeful selection of objects that vividly illustrate inevitability of bias
Effective organizational coherence with clearly signaled object sections
weaknesses: Insufficient provenance details (dates, locations, sources) for multiple objects and images
Inconsistent and disruptive reference formatting and integration
Analysis occasionally remains descriptive rather than fully exploring TOK concepts or ways of knowing
Uneven depth across objects (notably the marketing example) and some repetition
Abrupt ending lacking a synthesized conclusion tying back to the IA prompt
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition demonstrates almost all the qualities of the highest band. The student identifies three well-contextualized, specific objects and maintains a clear, prompt-focused analysis throughout. Most claims are illustrated with concrete examples and supported by citations, and the rationale for each objectâs inclusion is convincing. Minor limitationsâsuch as missing provenance details, inconsistent referencing, uneven analytical depth, limited TOK terminology, and an abrupt conclusionâprevent a perfect score but do not seriously undermine the overall coherence and insight of the work. Awarding 9 out of 10 accurately reflects the strong performance across the rubricâs requirements.

annotations.comment: The student correctly identifies and names âconfirmation bias,â reflecting a solid connection to cognitive TOK terminology in the discussion of marketing strategies.

The header identifies the first object but lacks precise context details such as date, location and media source. Specify the courtroom jurisdiction, trial dates and broadcast channel to anchor this object in its real-world context.

The analysis of the Depp vs. Heard case remains largely descriptive. To deepen the TOK focus, the student should link observations explicitly to the IA prompt and explore how specific ways of knowing (e.g., emotion, reason) contribute to juror bias.

Footnotes 1 and 2 are placed immediately after the second header, disrupting the readerâs flow. Integrate references at the point of citation within the text and consider using a unified bibliography.
The section on Pepsi vs. Coca-Cola effectively illustrates how marketing campaigns leverage confirmation bias, demonstrating a real-world manifestation of the IA prompt.
While the marketing battle is well described, it lacks precise contextâsuch as campaign dates, geographic markets and source citations. Include these details to strengthen object provenance.
The Pepsi ad image is pertinent but unreferenced. Provide the campaignâs release date, advertising agency or URL to situate this visual as a specific real-world object.
Similarly, the Coca-Cola image lacks provenance (date, medium, source). Detailing these aspects will reinforce its validity as a TOK object.
The student directly quotes the IA prompt as the title, ensuring clear alignment with the task from the outset and immediately focusing the exhibition on the knowledge production question.

The introduction summarizes bias broadly but does not frame a clear knowledge question or explicitly use TOK terminology (e.g., ways of knowing, knowledge frameworks). The student should refine the question and integrate TOK concepts here.

The composite image is evocative but the commentary does not explain how media framing of this visual influenced viewersâ knowledge. Cite specific frames or editing choices and analyse their biasing effect.
Object labels switch between âexampleâ and âobject,â which may confuse the exhibitionâs structure. Use consistent terminology (e.g., â1st object,â â2nd objectâ) to maintain clarity.
This concluding paragraph on objectivity in the BLM context reads as an afterthought and does not fully synthesise with earlier analysis. A stronger conclusion is needed to tie back to the IA prompt.
The Black Lives Matter statue is a strong choice, but its location, installation date and sculptor are not identified. Include these to contextualise its significance.
The third object header clearly signals a new TOK object (BLM movements), maintaining the exhibitionâs organizational coherence.

The analysis of BLM protests describes media framing but remains surface-level. The student should explicitly link these examples back to the IA prompt and explore how different ways of knowing shape public perceptions.
The final sentence of this section is truncated. Ensure complete sentences and thought progression to maintain analytical clarity.
The protest photograph is evocative but lacks provenance details (photographer, date, location). Providing these will ground it as a specific real-world knowledge object.
The bibliography lists only three sources and omits references for the advertising and image examples. Add full citations for each campaign and visual to support object credibility.
Citation formats vary (online articles vs. press releases). Standardise to a single style (e.g., APA or MLA) to enhance academic rigor.
A dedicated conclusion is missing before the bibliography. The student should add a final section that synthesises the three objects and reinforces the IA promptâs implications.
Footnote 3 employs a different citation style than earlier notes. Adopt a consistent referencing format throughout the exhibition or consolidate references in the bibliography.
The exhibition ends abruptly without a concluding section that synthesises insights from all three objects. A summary drawing together the implications for bias in knowledge production is essential.
The bibliography is clearly titled and separate, demonstrating an understanding of academic convention for listing sources.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
